q: You want a daily 7am summary posted to Discord. Which pattern fits best?
explain: Cron is the right tool for a single recurring task with no human review. Sub-agents die with the session; Kanban is overkill for one job.

q: What is the difference between a Kanban assignee profile and a sub-agent?
choices: They are the same thing with different names
Profiles are separate Hermes instances with their own config and specialisation; sub-agents are ephemeral and live inside one session
Sub-agents have their own API keys
explain: Profiles persist across sessions with their own API keys, working directory, and specialisation. Sub-agents are spawned on demand by the orchestrator and have no persistent state.

q: Short answer: name one habit that prevents cron jobs from silently failing.
explain: Any of: log to a file and check it; test the Skill manually before scheduling; verify the scheduler is running (`hermes cron status`); use absolute paths; schedule a canary heartbeat job.

q: Per the source video, why should you NOT pair Kanban with cron right now?
choices: Kanban and cron use the same database
Kanban does not have reliable delete and dedup logic yet
Cron jobs always overwrite Kanban state
explain: Per `R_aLVXYzDac`, pairing the two caused duplicate tasks and race conditions until Hermes ships reliable delete and dedup. Run them separately for now.

q: Which model class is appropriate for sub-agent delegation?
explain: Per the L06 source video, parallel research with the cheapest model took 14 minutes — longer than doing it directly. Reserve sub-agents for Sonnet-class or better.

q: True or false: a cron job scheduled inside Hermes survives an `hermes` process restart.
choices: True — cron entries are stored on disk and re-loaded
False — cron is tied to the running Hermes process
explain: Cron entries are persisted by `hermes cron create` and re-loaded on startup. The scheduler daemon is independent of any single chat session, but it is part of the Hermes process — keep Hermes running (L07 covers systemd / launchd setups).
